input.background: An environmental researcher wishes to measure the plant diversity in an area of $100 \times 100 \mathrm{~m}$ in a meadow. She decides to take 100 readings using a 1 m quadrat, and considers the following three survey designs.
input.question: Identify which of the designs is random, systematic or stratified-random, and state briefly any main advantages and disadvantages of the method: (i) Dividing the sample area into ten 10 m blocks and taking one sample from a random coordinate within each of the blocks.

output.answer:
(i) Stratified-random.

Advantage: incorporates the advantages of both of the methods below.

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
